title: Create E2E test: backspace input handling
notes: Type 'git status', backspace to clear, type 'echo hello world', verify output. Tests that backspace is properly forwarded to PTY. Reference: tui-testing RESEARCH_SUMMARY.md backspace test.

title: Review: UI Overhaul Phase 1 - State Machine and Focus Management
description:
Final review for UI overhaul Phase 1:
- Verify all implementation issues are closed
- Run all tests (unit, integration, E2E)
- Verify no tests are skipped
- Check for any warnings or clippy issues
- Verify all spec requirements from objectives.md are met:
  * State machine with focus and mode management
  * Hint bar with dynamic keybindings and quit path
  * Sidebar session list with selection and scrolling
  * All keybindings work as specified
  * Spec 256-colors applied
  * Confirmation prompts for delete and quit
  * Create and rename modes with text input
  * Welcome state for first-time startup
- Visual inspection in Apple Terminal
- Re-install sb binary for user testing

title: Implement terminal state serialization for session restore
description:
Add terminal state serialization to the daemon for restoring sessions on reconnect:
1. Daemon maintains vt100 parser state (already have vt100 crate)
2. All PTY output fed to vt100 parser for state tracking
3. On client reconnect, serialize terminal state from vt100 screen buffer
4. Send serialized state to client to restore screen contents
5. Include cursor position, colors, and text attributes in serialization

Reference: zmx serializeTerminalState in references/session-persistence/zmx/src/main.zig line 1505
Reference: shpool session_restore in references/session-persistence/shpool/libshpool/src/session_restore/mod.rs

title: Integrate session list with daemon for create/delete/rename operations
description:
Connect AppState to daemon for session management:
- Load session list from daemon on startup
- Create session: send Attach message with new name to daemon
- Delete session: send Kill message to daemon
- Rename session: need to add new daemon message type for rename
- Update DaemonClient with rename_session() method
- Add ClientMessage::Rename and DaemonResponse handling
- Update session list when operations complete
- Handle welcome state (no sessions) properly
- Integration tests with daemon

Reference: src/daemon.rs for existing protocol

title: Implement HintBar module with line wrapping and quit path
description:
Create src/hint_bar.rs with:
- KeybindingInfo struct (key, description, enabled)
- HintBarMode enum (Normal, Confirm, Message)
- HintBar struct with bindings, mode, quit_path, height
- Line wrapping logic that never splits a keybinding across lines
- calculate_height() method for dynamic layout
- render() method with right-aligned quit path and separator
- Spec colors: purple (165) for keys, white (255) for descriptions, dark grey (238) bg, dark red (88) for important
- Unit tests for wrapping at various widths

Reference: references/hint-bar/RESEARCH_SUMMARY.md

title: Create Rust project scaffold with ratatui/crossterm
notes: Initialize Cargo.toml with dependencies: ratatui, crossterm, color-eyre. Create basic main.rs that initializes terminal, runs event loop, and handles Ctrl+Q to quit. Reference: ratatui/examples/apps/hello-world/

title: Integrate portable-pty for spawning shell
notes: Add portable-pty dependency. Create PTY pair, spawn shell (bash/zsh) with current working directory. Set up reader thread to pipe PTY output to main thread via channel. Reference: portable-pty examples/bash.rs, terminal-emulation RESEARCH_SUMMARY.md.

title: E2E test: Session persistence across TUI restart
description:
Add E2E test for session persistence:
1. Start TUI and open vi editing a file
2. Exit TUI (Ctrl+Q) without saving vi
3. Restart TUI
4. Verify vi is still open with the same content
5. Verify can save and exit vi normally

This tests the core session persistence functionality required by objectives.

title: Implement sidebar session list rendering with selection and scrolling
description:
Update sidebar rendering in main.rs:
- Replace static 'Sidebar TUI' with session list
- Render session names with line wrapping (│ and └ characters for continuation)
- Selection highlight with dark purple (56) background
- Truncation indicators (...) at top/bottom when list overflows
- Focus-aware border colors: white (255) when focused, dark grey (238) when not
- Title 'Sidebar TUI' in purple (165), left-aligned
- Welcome state text when no sessions exist
- Unit tests for rendering all states

Reference: objectives.md Sidebar Pane section

title: Review: Basic TUI with terminal emulation implementation
notes: Review all implementation issues. Verify: all tests pass, code is clean, no skipped tests, E2E tests run in Apple terminal, Ctrl+Q quits properly, layout matches spec exactly.

title: Implement AppState and Focus enums for state management
description:
Create src/state.rs with:
- Focus enum (Sidebar, Terminal)
- AppMode enum with state variants (Normal, CreateMode, Drafting, Renaming, Confirming)
- Supporting structs: DraftingState, RenamingState, ConfirmState, ConfirmAction
- Session struct for sidebar list items (name, is_selected, is_attached)
- AppState struct with focus, mode, sessions list, selected_session, scroll_offset, previous_session
- Default implementations and unit tests

Reference: references/state-management/RESEARCH_SUMMARY.md

title: Implement create and rename modes with text input
description:
Add text input handling for session names:
- Create mode shows t/a/esc options in hint bar
- Drafting state adds empty row to sidebar with blinking cursor
- Character restrictions: letters, numbers, spaces, -, _, . only
- Backspace to delete characters
- Enter to confirm and create session
- Esc to cancel and remove draft row
- Rename mode pre-fills with current name, cursor at end
- Terminal pane blank and non-interactive during drafting
- Cursor rendering for text input
- Unit tests for input validation

Reference: objectives.md Create Mode section
close_reason: Implemented create and rename modes with text input: (1) Added cursor rendering integration in main.rs using get_sidebar_cursor_position() for drafting/renaming modes, (2) Terminal pane now shows blank during drafting mode with unfocused border, (3) Hint bar already shows correct context-aware bindings for create/drafting/renaming modes, (4) Character restrictions and input handling were already implemented in state.rs and input_handler.rs. Added 6 new unit tests for drafting/renaming mode behaviors. All 56 tests pass.

title: E2E tests for UI overhaul Phase 1
description:
Add E2E tests for new UI functionality:
- test_sidebar_session_list: verify session list renders with selection
- test_hint_bar_context: verify hint bar shows correct bindings for each state
- test_focus_switching: verify ctrl+b switches focus, border colors change
- test_create_mode_flow: verify n enters create mode, t creates session
- test_rename_flow: verify r enters rename mode, enter confirms
- test_delete_confirmation: verify d shows prompt, y deletes, n cancels
- test_quit_confirmation: verify q shows prompt, y quits, n cancels
- test_navigation: verify ↑/↓ moves selection, scroll with many sessions
- test_welcome_state: verify welcome message when no sessions

Reference: tests/e2e_tests.rs for existing E2E patterns

title: Implement keyboard event handling with state machine
description:
Refactor event loop in main.rs:
- Create handle_key() method that checks mode first, then focus
- Sidebar focus keybindings: enter/space/→ select, ↑/↓ navigate, esc jump back, n new, d delete, r rename, q quit
- Terminal focus keybindings: ctrl+b/ctrl+t focus sidebar, ctrl+n new
- Create mode keybindings: t terminal session, a agent session, esc cancel
- Drafting mode keybindings: character input, backspace, enter create, esc cancel
- Renaming mode keybindings: same as drafting
- Confirming mode keybindings: y yes, n no
- EventResult enum (Consumed, NotConsumed, Quit)
- Unit tests for state transitions

Reference: references/state-management/RESEARCH_SUMMARY.md, objectives.md keybinding sections

title: Move PTY ownership from TUI to daemon
description:
Refactor the TUI to become a client that connects to the session daemon instead of directly owning the PTY:
1. Move PTY creation from main.rs to daemon
2. Daemon spawns shell and owns PTY read/write
3. TUI connects to daemon via Unix socket
4. Daemon forwards PTY output to all connected clients
5. TUI sends keyboard input to daemon, daemon forwards to PTY

Reference: zmx client/daemon IPC in references/session-persistence/zmx/src/ipc.zig
Reference: shpool attach in references/session-persistence/shpool/libshpool/src/attach.rs

title: Clone and study Rust terminal multiplexer projects
notes: Clone the source code of existing Rust terminal projects that do similar things to what we're building. Focus on: zellij (terminal multiplexer), wezterm (terminal emulator), rio (terminal emulator). Study their architecture for embedding terminals within a TUI. Save to references/existing-projects/

title: Implement input forwarding from crossterm to PTY
notes: Convert crossterm KeyEvent to terminal escape sequences. Handle special keys: arrows (ESC[A/B/C/D), backspace (0x7f), enter (0x0d), Ctrl+C (0x03), tab. Forward to PTY writer. Reference: zellij terminal_pane.rs key constants.

title: Add session management commands (list, kill, attach)
description:
Add session management functionality:
1. List active sessions: show session names, PIDs, creation time
2. Kill sessions: terminate daemon and shell process
3. Attach to existing session: connect TUI client to running daemon
4. Auto-create session on first run if none exists
5. Session naming convention for sidebar threading

Reference: zmx list/kill in references/session-persistence/zmx/src/main.zig
Reference: shpool list/kill in references/session-persistence/shpool/libshpool/src/list.rs and kill.rs

title: Implement confirmation prompts for delete and quit
description:
Add confirmation prompt handling:
- Delete confirmation: 'Delete this session?' with y/n options, red background (88)
- Quit confirmation: 'Quit Sidebar TUI?' with y/n options, red background (88)
- Update hint bar to show prompt message with bindings
- Handle y/n key events in Confirming mode
- Cancel with any other key returns to previous state
- Update quit path on right side of hint bar based on current state
- Unit tests for confirmation flow

Reference: objectives.md Sidebar Pane d and q keybinding sections

title: Research: ANSI 256-color support in ratatui for spec colors
description: Research how to use specific ANSI 256 colors in ratatui. The spec calls for specific colors: purple (165), white (255), dark grey (238), dark purple (56), dark red (88). Need to verify how to use Color::Indexed(n) and test that these render correctly in Apple Terminal.

title: Build CLI and link globally as 'sb'
notes: Configure Cargo.toml [[bin]] section with name 'sb'. Build release binary. Install globally with cargo install --path . or symlink to PATH. Verify 'sb' command works from any directory.

title: Create E2E test: layout matches spec
notes: Add terminal-testlib dev dependency. Test that sidebar is 20 chars wide, title row is blue with centered 'Sidebar TUI' in black, sidebar body has lighter background. Reference: tui-testing RESEARCH_SUMMARY.md test examples.

title: Research terminal emulation libraries in Rust
notes: Research and clone docs/code for Rust libraries that handle terminal emulation and PTY management. Focus on: portable-pty, vt100, termwiz, pty-process. Need to understand how to create a pseudo-terminal, run shell commands, and capture/render output. Save to references/terminal-emulation/

title: Handle terminal resize events
notes: Detect crossterm Event::Resize. Update PTY size with master.resize(PtySize). Update vt100 parser dimensions with parser.set_size(). Recalculate layout constraints. Reference: wezterm resize handling.

title: Add vt100 parser for terminal emulation
notes: Add vt100 dependency. Create Parser with terminal dimensions. Feed PTY output to parser. Implement rendering from vt100::Screen to ratatui Paragraph widget with proper color mapping. Reference: vt100 examples, terminal-emulation RESEARCH_SUMMARY.md.

title: Implement dynamic hint bar rendering in layout
description:
Integrate hint bar into main layout:
- Add HintBar to DaemonApp/render state
- Calculate hint bar height before layout split
- Use Layout::vertical with dynamic Constraint::Length for hint bar
- Render hint bar at bottom of screen
- Update bindings based on current app state and focus
- Update quit path based on current state
- Temporary message support with timer-based dismissal
- Visual verification that hint bar grows/shrinks correctly
- Unit tests for layout calculations

Reference: references/hint-bar/RESEARCH_SUMMARY.md

title: Add Ctrl+B as alternate quit keybinding (objectives require both Ctrl+Q and Ctrl+B to quit)
Plan: UI Overhaul Phase 1 - State Machine and Focus Management
description:
After research is complete, plan the implementation of a proper state machine for the TUI. Current implementation has a simple event loop with no focus states. Need to support:
- SidebarFocused vs TerminalFocused
- CreateMode (showing create options)
- DraftingSession (typing new session name)
- RenamingSession (typing to rename)
- DeleteConfirmation (y/n prompt)
- QuitConfirmation (y/n prompt)
- WelcomeState (no sessions yet)

This is the foundation for all other UI work.

title: Create E2E test: vi editing workflow
notes: Open vi in TUI terminal, edit a file, save it, exit. Verify file contents changed. Test insert mode, escape, :wq sequence. Reference: tui-testing RESEARCH_SUMMARY.md vi test example.

title: Implement sidebar layout with 20-char fixed width
notes: Create Layout::horizontal with Constraint::Length(20) for sidebar and Fill(1) for terminal area. Add blue header row with 'Sidebar TUI' centered in black text. Sidebar body should have a lighter background color. Reference: ratatui demo ui.rs, constraints example.

title: Research E2E testing strategies for TUI applications
notes: Figure out how agents can automatically test a TUI that requires user input and interaction. Research: expectrl, rexpect, termion testing, scripted terminal automation. Need to test in a real Apple terminal environment. The objectives explicitly say to figure this out BEFORE building the TUI. Save to references/tui-testing/

title: Create session daemon module with Unix socket server
description:
Create src/daemon.rs module that implements a session daemon:
1. Unix socket server per session (one daemon per terminal session)
2. Socket file location in XDG_RUNTIME_DIR or /tmp/sidebar-tui-{uid}/
3. Daemon process that forks and detaches from TUI
4. Session naming for identifying/reconnecting to sessions
5. Message protocol for communication between TUI client and daemon
6. Signal handling for graceful shutdown (SIGTERM, SIGHUP)

Reference: zmx architecture in references/session-persistence/zmx/src/main.zig (Daemon struct)
Reference: shpool daemon in references/session-persistence/shpool/libshpool/src/daemon/

title: Add session persistence across reboots
description:
Persist session metadata to disk for survival across system reboots:
1. Save session metadata (name, command, cwd) to disk on creation
2. Metadata location in XDG_DATA_HOME or ~/.local/share/sidebar-tui/
3. On TUI startup, check for persisted sessions and offer to restore
4. Optionally auto-restore sessions on startup
5. Clean up stale session files for sessions that no longer exist

Note: Per objectives, user wants to pick up vi editing where they left off. Full process state cannot survive reboot, but command can be re-run with warning like Zellij does.

Reference: Zellij session serialization in references/existing-projects/zellij/zellij-utils/src/session_serialization.rs

title: Research Rust TUI frameworks (ratatui, crossterm, cursive)
notes:
## Research Complete

**Recommendation: Use ratatui + crossterm**

### Cloned Repositories:
- ratatui: references/tui-frameworks/ratatui/
- crossterm: references/tui-frameworks/crossterm/
- cursive: references/tui-frameworks/cursive/

### Key Findings:
1. **ratatui** is the best choice for building the TUI layout (sidebar + terminal view)
2. Uses crossterm as backend for cross-platform support
3. Layout system with Constraint::Length(20) matches our sidebar requirement perfectly
4. Ctrl+Q detection via crossterm's KeyModifiers::CONTROL + KeyCode::Char('q')

### Relevant Examples Reviewed:
- ratatui/examples/apps/hello-world/ - Basic app structure
- ratatui/examples/apps/demo/src/ui.rs - Complex layouts with sidebars
- ratatui/examples/apps/constraints/ - All constraint types
- crossterm/examples/event-match-modifiers.rs - Ctrl+key handling

### Summary Document:
See references/tui-frameworks/RESEARCH_SUMMARY.md for detailed implementation notes and code patterns.

title: Apply spec colors to all UI components
description:
Replace basic ANSI colors with spec 256-colors:
- Title: purple (165)
- Session names: white (255)
- Wrap indicators (│, └): dark grey (238)
- Selected session background: dark purple (56)
- Truncation indicator (...): dark grey (238)
- Focused border: white (255)
- Unfocused border: dark grey (238)
- Terminal text: white (255)
- Hint bar background: dark grey (238)
- Important prompt background: dark red (88)
- Keybindings in hint bar: purple (165)
- Welcome text: grey (238) with purple keybinding
- Update existing unit tests for new colors
- Visual verification with examples/color_test.rs

Reference: src/colors.rs for constants, objectives.md for color spec
close_reason: All spec colors are already applied throughout the codebase: colors.rs defines PURPLE/WHITE/DARK_GREY/DARK_PURPLE/DARK_RED constants, sidebar.rs uses them for title/session names/wrap indicators/selection/borders, hint_bar.rs uses them for background/keybindings/confirmation prompts, and main.rs uses them for terminal view borders. Unit tests verify color application.

title: Research session persistence approaches for terminal apps
description:
Investigate approaches for persisting terminal sessions across TUI restarts and even reboots:
1. tmux integration - running shell sessions inside tmux and reattaching
2. screen integration - similar to tmux
3. CRIU (Checkpoint/Restore) - Linux-specific kernel feature
4. PTY detachment patterns from zellij/wezterm
5. What limitations exist for each approach

Goal: Determine the best approach for making vi/vim editing sessions survive TUI close and reopen, even across reboots. Clone relevant docs/code to references/ folder.
close_reason: Completed research on session persistence approaches. Cloned tmux, tmux-resurrect, zmx, and shpool to references/session-persistence/. Recommendation: Implement zmx/shpool-style daemon architecture. See RESEARCH_SUMMARY.md for full details.

title: Research: TUI state management patterns for complex multi-pane focus
description: Research how to implement complex TUI state management with multiple focus states (sidebar focused, terminal focused, create mode, rename mode, delete confirmation, etc.). Look at how ratatui examples and similar projects handle this. Clone relevant examples to references/.
notes:
## Research Complete

Cloned gitui and bottom projects to references/state-management/ and analyzed state management patterns from:
- Ratatui examples (demo2, user-input, popup)
- Zellij InputMode enum
- gitui Component trait and Focus management
- bottom ProcessKillDialog state machine

Created comprehensive RESEARCH_SUMMARY.md with recommended patterns:
1. Primary Focus enum (Sidebar vs Terminal)
2. AppMode enum with state variants (Normal, CreateMode, Drafting, Renaming, Confirming)
3. Modal state handling takes precedence over focus-based handling
4. EventResult enum for event consumption/bubbling
5. Previous focus tracking for 'jump back' on Esc

Key references:
- references/state-management/gitui/ - Component trait with focused()/focus() methods, EventState enum
- references/state-management/bottom/ - ProcessKillDialog state machine, AppSearchState for text input
- references/state-management/RESEARCH_SUMMARY.md - Full documentation with code examples

title: Create E2E test: git status output matches normal terminal
notes: Run 'git status' in TUI terminal. Compare output with baseline from running git status directly. Test in the project directory which is a git repo. Reference: tui-testing RESEARCH_SUMMARY.md.

title: Review: Session persistence implementation
description:
Final review of session persistence implementation:
1. Verify all 6 implementation issues are complete
2. Verify E2E test passes for session persistence
3. Verify all existing tests still pass
4. Verify the objective is met: 'terminal session should be automatically saved and restored when reopening the TUI'
5. Test manually: start vi, edit file, quit TUI, restart TUI, verify vi is still there

title: Research: Ratatui hint bar / status bar implementations
description: Research how to implement a dynamic hint bar that shows keybindings, can wrap to multiple lines, shows quit path on the right side, and can display confirmation prompts with colored backgrounds. Look at ratatui examples and popular TUI apps like lazygit, bottom, etc. Clone relevant references.

title: Research tmux Rust integration libraries
description:
Research Rust libraries and approaches for integrating with tmux:
1. tmux command-line interface wrapper
2. tmux control mode (-CC flag)
3. Existing Rust tmux crates
4. How to spawn shells inside tmux sessions
5. How to list/reattach to existing sessions
6. How other terminal multiplexers (zellij) handle persistence

Clone relevant code examples and documentation to references/session-persistence/ folder.
close_reason: Superseded by session persistence research (sidebar_tui-uzt). Decided to implement zmx/shpool-style daemon architecture instead of tmux integration. This approach uses our existing vt100 and portable-pty crates, requires no external dependencies, and provides full terminal state restoration.
